food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — fried chicken burger, french fries, and soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for McChicken Upsized Meal combo. FOODON_00002490 (chicken sandwich food product) is the closest defensible anchor for the burger component. The full meal includes fries and a beverage which are not captured by this single code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers the chicken burger component. No single FoodEx2 code covers the full upsized combo meal (burger + large fries + large drink). Code applied to the primary burger component as the dominant food item.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for a fast food chicken burger combo meal. SNOMED CT clinical food coding does not extend to branded fast food combo products at this level of specificity.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (McChicken burger + large fries + large soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One McChicken Upsized Meal consists of one McChicken burger (approximately 160 g), one large portion of french fries (approximately 170 g), and one large soft drink (approximately 350 ml / 350 g), totalling approximately 680 g per meal as served.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — McChicken Burger, French Fries (Large), Soft Drink (Large); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried chicken sandwich, fast food french fries, and cola beverage
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (primary product data); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (secondary cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100 g are derived by combining component weights: McChicken burger (~160 g), large fries (~170 g), large cola (~350 g), total ~680 g. McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data provides per-item values for each component. Per-100g values are calculated by weighted aggregation across the three components. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries (Chicken sandwich, fast food, NDB 21120; French fries, fast food, NDB 21137; Carbonated beverage, cola, NDB 14400) were used for cross-validation and to fill micronutrient fields not published by McDonald's Singapore. Values represent the full upsized meal as a single composite food record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fast food composite meals including fried chicken burger and french fries; Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McChicken Upsized Meal as a composite product. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, using analogous fast food composite meal entries: white bread bun (GI ~71), fried chicken patty (GI ~46 for breaded fried chicken), french fries (GI ~63 for fast food fries), and cola (GI ~63). Weighted average by carbohydrate contribution across components yields a composite GI of approximately 65 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 166.6 g carbohydrate in 680 g meal) / 100 ≈ 108.5. Carbohydrate per serving = 24.5 g per 100 g × 6.8 (serving multiplier) = 166.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 1326 mg sodium per full upsized meal serving (195 mg per 100 g × 680 g), representing over 55% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of the McChicken Upsized Meal due to its very high sodium load from the burger, fries, and condiments; if consumed, it should replace all other high-sodium foods for that day and be paired with potassium-rich foods to partially offset the sodium effect.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL ≈ 108.5) driven by refined carbohydrates from the burger bun, french fries, and sugar-sweetened cola, combined with high total carbohydrate content (~167 g per meal)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid the McChicken Upsized Meal as a regular food choice due to its very high glycemic load and large refined carbohydrate content, which will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed occasionally, the cola should be substituted with water or a diet beverage and portion size should be reduced to the regular (non-upsized) meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high saturated fat (~12.2 g per meal), trans fat (~0.7 g per meal), high sodium (~1326 mg per meal), and ultra-processed food classification, all of which are independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat the McChicken Upsized Meal as an occasional indulgence only, given its high saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium content; regular consumption is inconsistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns such as the DASH or Mediterranean diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The McChicken Upsized Meal is a fast food combo meal sold at McDonald's restaurants in Singapore and globally. It consists of a McChicken burger — a deep-fried breaded chicken patty served in a soft white enriched-flour bun with shredded lettuce and mayonnaise — accompanied by a large portion of salted deep-fried french fries and a large sugar-sweetened carbonated soft drink (typically cola). The meal is certified Halal in Singapore. The chicken patty is seasoned, coated in a light breadcrumb batter, and deep-fried, yielding a crispy exterior with a tender, juicy interior. The mayonnaise adds a creamy, mildly tangy flavour. The fries are thin-cut, salted, and crispy. The full upsized meal weighs approximately 680 g and delivers around 1190 kcal, making it a high-calorie, high-carbohydrate, high-sodium meal. Nutritionally, the meal is energy-dense with approximately 175 kcal per 100 g, 5.1 g protein, 6.8 g fat (including 1.8 g saturated fat), and 24.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g. The sodium content is clinically significant at approximately 195 mg per 100 g, totalling over 1300 mg per meal. The glycemic index of the composite meal is estimated at 65 (medium GI), but the glycemic load per serving is very high at approximately 108.5 due to the large carbohydrate volume from the bun, fries, and cola. This meal is classified as ultra-processed and is not recommended for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease as a regular dietary choice. It is best consumed as an occasional treat within an otherwise balanced diet.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a McChicken Upsized Meal?
Is the McChicken Upsized Meal Halal in Singapore?
Is McChicken meal good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived by weighted aggregation of McDonald's Singapore published per-item nutrition data for the McChicken burger, large fries, and large soft drink components, cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for analogous fast food items. McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data publicly, but does not publish a single composite per-100g figure for the upsized meal as a whole. The aggregation method introduces minor uncertainty. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, as no direct GI measurement exists for this specific composite meal. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values per 100 g are calculated by weighted aggregation: McChicken burger ~160 g, large fries ~170 g, large cola ~350 g, total ~680 g. Component weights are based on McDonald's Singapore published serving sizes. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in McDonald's Singapore nutrition data or the USDA SR Legacy cross-reference entries used for this composite meal. 3. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous fast food entries (NDB 21120, 21137) as McDonald's Singapore does not publish these sub-fractions publicly; values are approximate. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for the McChicken Upsized Meal composite exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. The meal is Halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore locations. 6. Price data from field survey data input: SGD 8.70 (fixed price across both listed outlets). 7. The food_id GD000142 is preserved from the input seed record. 8. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g reflect nutrients not reported in the primary or cross-reference sources used for this composite meal record.
